title: Ground Communication
description: Learn to communicate with ground control: taxi clearances and readbacks.

content.definition: Set your transponder to a specific 4-digit code assigned by ATC.
Repeat the key parts of an ATC clearance back to the controller to confirm you understood correctly.
content.example_atc: N172SP, taxi to runway 28 via Alpha, hold short of runway 10.
content.example_response: Taxi to runway 28 via Alpha, hold short of runway 10, N172SP.

content.atc_text: N172SP, taxi to runway 28 via Alpha, hold short of runway 10.
content.question: Where are you instructed to hold short?
content.explanation: ATC said 'hold short of runway 10' — you must stop before crossing runway 10.

content.prompt: N172SP, taxi to _____ 28 via _____, hold short of runway 10.
content.context: ATC is giving you a taxi clearance to runway 28 via taxiway Alpha.

content.atc_text: N172SP, taxi to runway 28 via Alpha, hold short of runway 10.
content.correct_response: Taxi to runway 28 via Alpha, hold short of runway 10, N172SP.
content.hint: Read back the taxi route and hold short instruction, then say your callsign.
acceptable_variants: Runway 28 via Alpha, hold short runway 10, N172SP.
Taxi runway 28 Alpha, hold short of 10, N172SP.

content.question: What should you do next?
options: Switch to frequency 121.9 and call ground control
content.explanation: When told to 'contact ground 121.9', tune your radio to 121.9 MHz and call ground control.

content.hint: Acknowledge by repeating the frequency and your callsign.
